title: Executive Order 12740—Waiver Under the Trade Act of 1974 With Respect to the Soviet Union

flags.justification: Exercises a specific, narrow, congressionally-provided waiver mechanism (Trade Act §402(c)(2)), a bounded and anticipated exercise, not a novel executive claim.
The order confirms the congressionally required precondition report was already made, demonstrating real, satisfied accountability.
A narrow statutory waiver, not a compliance regime.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
Jackson-Vanik-related waivers under this section are a periodically-renewed mechanism, evergreen by design via its own statutory reporting and renewal requirement.
The required congressional reporting precondition adds rather than removes oversight.
No third-party compliance regime is created.
A routine, narrow, congressionally-anticipated waiver exercise; no novel authority claim.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. A clean, well-designed, congressionally-anticipated waiver mechanism related to Jackson-Vanik trade status.
